title: Bulk report - Week 27
summary:
Capesize
As the week commenced in the Pacific, it became apparent that there was a notable level of enquiry, defying the usual subdued nature of Mondays. The majors from West Australia to China have been present in the market throughout the week. However, the market has not been immune to the impact of the increasing tonnage supply as noted by brokers.

children.title: Capesize
children.summary: As the week commenced in the Pacific, it became apparent that there was a notable level of enquiry, defying the usual subdued nature of Mondays. The majors from West Australia to China have been present in the market throughout the week.

children.title: Panamax
children.summary: The Panamax market began the week in a slow and depressive state. Despite the North Atlantic offering reasonable fresh demand, this made insignificant impact to rates with owners keen to take quick cover, discouraging any hope of any rate revival.

children.title: Ultramax/Supramax
children.summary: Overall, a poor week for the sector dominated by a strong supply of prompt tonnage and limited fresh enquiry. As the week ended the US Gulf bucked the trend of strong numbers being discussed as a light and more optimistic outlook appeared.

children.title: Handysize
children.summary: The issues with a lack of fresh enquiry and general activity across both basins continued this week and the handy sector's negative sentiment remained. East Coast South America numbers continued to soften with a 36,000-dwt fixing from Rosario to Denmark at a rate between $11,500 to $12,000 whilst a 34,000-dwt also opening in Rosario was fixed to the US Gulf with an intended cargo of petcoke at $13,000.
